target_case: AAPL-like mega-cap platform; HIMS-like consumer/product mid-cap; one failed growth story; one cyclical/macro-sensitive company
expected_increment: Integrate consumer, product, macro, industry, company and valuation expectations into one long-term thesis map with explicit conflicts and refresh triggers.
falsification_condition: If it does not improve over ordinary long_term_thesis in identifying the weakest assumption, market-implied expectations and falsification triggers, do not adopt.

target_case: consumer brand, marketplace, subscription, healthcare consumer access case
expected_increment: Prevent demand claims from relying only on TAM or management commentary by checking adoption path, affordability, substitution, retention and channel behavior.
falsification_condition: If public or primary evidence cannot support demand variables beyond generic market growth, keep as optional only.

target_case: software, platform, consumer tech, device, medical product case
expected_increment: Test whether product actually solves a customer job and passes value/usability/feasibility/viability risks.
falsification_condition: If it routinely becomes qualitative product praise without measurable adoption or retention evidence, retire or narrow.

target_case: high-duration growth, cyclical, export chain, financial, consumer discretionary case
expected_increment: Translate macro regime into revenue, margin, financing, discount-rate, valuation and positioning channels.
falsification_condition: If it frequently misclassifies company/industry questions as macro questions, keep macro as overlay only.

target_case: semiconductor supply chain, consumer platform, industrial, healthcare services case
expected_increment: Map profit pool, supplier/customer power, competitive intensity, regulation, technology route and industry life cycle.
falsification_condition: If it produces static Porter-style summaries without identifying the changing profit pool, revise.

target_case: compounder, turnaround, early-stage growth, capital-intensive industrial case
expected_increment: Convert company quality into ROIC, reinvestment runway, management capital allocation, financial quality and execution evidence.
falsification_condition: If it over-penalizes early-stage firms or misses dilution/survival risks, split by maturity stage.

expected_increment: Reverse-engineer market-implied growth, margin, ROIC, duration and risk-premium assumptions before judging opportunity.
falsification_condition: If proxies are too weak and create fake precision, require confidence labels and ranges instead of point estimates.
